chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الرَّمْيِ عَنِ الْعَلِيلِ وَ الصِّبْيَانِ وَ الرَّمْيِ رَاكِباً
chapter_title.en: Chapter 177 – The pelting (of the Jamarah) on behalf of the sick, and the children, and the pelting while riding

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/155)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/155)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا إِبْرَاهِيمَ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْمَرِيضِ يُرْمَى عَنْهُ الْجِمَارُ قَالَ نَعَمْ يُحْمَلُ إِلَى الْجَمْرَةِ وَ يُرْمَى عَنْهُ .

en.hubeali: Abu Ali Al Ashary, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, from Safwan Bin Yahya, from Is’haq Bin Ammar who said,

‘I asked Abu Ibrahimasws (7th Imamasws about the sick, whether the Jamarah can be pelted on their behalf. Heasws said: ‘Yes, he would be carried to the Jamarah, and these would be pelted on his behalf’.
en.sarwar: 2. Abu Ali al-Ash’ariy has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd al-Jabbar from Safwan ibn Yahya from Ishaq ibn ‘Ammar who has said the following: “I once asked abu Ibrahim, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who is ill; if pebbles on al-Jamar can be thrown on his behalf. He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, he must be taken to al-Jamar and then pebbles are thrown on his behalf.’”
